user_query:
I'm a pro player with the River City Rockets, and I’m lining up posts to promote our interview show and engage fans. I’ve put two episode transcripts and rough descriptions in the workspace. Please do the following using only the provided files.

Inputs to use:
- Transcripts: all Markdown files under input/transcripts/*.md
- Rough descriptions: input/descriptions.csv

Deliverables:
1) Extracted episode summary (output/episodes_summary.jsonl)
   - For each transcript you process, write exactly one JSON object per line with these fields:
     - episode_id (string) — use the filename without extension, e.g., ep01
     - guest_name (string) — from the transcript header
     - team (string) — from the transcript header
     - tags (array of strings) — split from the header’s Tags line
     - pull_quote (object) with fields:
       - text (string) — the exact text from the line starting with "Pull quote (timestamp ...):"
       - timestamp (string) — the timestamp from that same pull-quote line
   - Use only the content in each transcript file to populate these fields.

2) Rewritten social posts (output/social_posts.csv)
   - For every episode_id listed in input/descriptions.csv, create two rows of promotional copy rewritten from the rough description, one for each platform: X and Instagram.
   - Output columns must be: episode_id, platform, post_text.
   - Requirements for post_text, per platform:
     - X: 
       - Max 280 characters.
       - Include the team name "River City Rockets" and the guest’s name.
       - Include the pull quote text exactly as extracted (enclosed in double quotes).
       - Include a clear call to action phrase: either "Listen now" or "Tune in" (choose one).
     - Instagram:
       - Max 300 characters.
       - Include the team name "River City Rockets" and the guest’s name.
       - Include the pull quote text exactly as extracted (enclosed in double quotes).
       - Include both hashtags: #RCRockets and #ClubhouseChats.
   - The posts must be upbeat and fan-friendly, rewriting the provided rough descriptions for clarity and brevity while integrating the exact pull quote and required elements.
   - The quote used in each post must match the pull_quote.text you wrote to output/episodes_summary.jsonl for the same episode.

3) Audit of inspected inputs (output/audit/inspected_transcripts.txt)
   - List each processed transcript path on its own line, then a final line of the form TOTAL=<number_of_transcripts>.

Process notes:
- Inspect the input/transcripts directory to discover all .md transcripts and process each one.
- Match episodes by episode_id (e.g., ep01) between transcripts and input/descriptions.csv.
- Do not create posts for episodes that are not present in input/descriptions.csv.
- Do not invent names, tags, or quotes not present in the inputs.

Output paths:
- output/episodes_summary.jsonl
- output/social_posts.csv
- output/audit/inspected_transcripts.txt All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
